message:
refactor(grpc): make CallContext clock-injected methods public and fix test determinism

CallContext had private from_metadata_at(), remaining_at(), and
is_expired_at() methods that accepted explicit Instant parameters.
These are now public so downstream code and replay harnesses can avoid
ambient Instant::now() calls.

Visibility changes in grpc/server.rs:
- from_metadata_at(): private → pub (with doc comment)
- remaining_at(): private → pub (with #[must_use])
- is_expired_at(): private → pub (with #[must_use])

Test determinism fixes in grpc_production_features.rs:
- call_context_from_metadata_with_timeout_header: use from_metadata_at()
  with pinned now, assert exact deadline instead of ±100ms range
- call_context_from_metadata_uses_default_timeout_when_no_header: same
- call_context_from_metadata_header_overrides_default: same
- call_context_remaining_before_deadline: use remaining_at(now) with
  exact Duration::from_secs(10) assertion
- call_context_remaining_after_deadline: use remaining_at(now)
- call_context_is_expired_with_past_deadline: use is_expired_at(now)
- call_context_is_expired_with_future_deadline: use is_expired_at(now)

All formerly flaky time-window assertions are now exact equality checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
